title: woof agent role configuration
description: Project-side declaration at .woof/agents.toml. Dispatchable roles resolve to public CLI adapters (claude or codex); Woof builds the subprocess invocation itself and does not require private shell wrappers. In trusted-local mode, Woof does not add runtime sandboxing, command allow-lists, writable-path restrictions, network policy, or MCP restriction logic; broad public-CLI permission modes are intentional. Roles may declare adapter-specific model, effort, flags, and Claude MCP server names. Legacy role names and legacy harness values are accepted only as migration input and are resolved to public adapters at runtime.

roles.description: Semantic roles invoked by Woof. primary and reviewer are the graph-owned dispatch roles. planner/story-executor/critiquer are legacy aliases retained for migration; planner and story-executor map to primary, critiquer maps to reviewer.

default_minutes.description: Per-subprocess timeout. The dispatch adapter wraps public CLI invocations with timeout. Hitting the timeout writes gate.md with triggered_by=['timeout'].

review_valve.description: Periodic review configuration (Stage 5 Check 9). Opens a review_gate every N completed stories AND once before epic close, surfacing accumulated minor critique findings.

every_n_stories.description: Open a review_gate after every N successful story commits.

end_of_epic.description: Open a review_gate after the last story completes, before epic close. Recommended on.

mcp_servers.description: Project-owned Claude MCP server definitions. Role mcp[] entries reference keys from this table. Woof renders these into --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config JSON; absent or empty mcp[] renders {"mcpServers":{}}.

audit.description: Audit policy for committed artefacts written by woof dispatch. Controls conservative redaction and the per-file size cap enforced before graph-owned commits.

enabled.description: Apply redaction and size cap before writing audit artefacts. Set to false to disable both filters (not recommended for production use).

max_bytes.description: Per-file size cap in bytes. Audit artefacts exceeding this are truncated at commit time; the full output overflows to the gitignored audit/raw/ path. Default 262144 (256 KB).

redact_patterns.description: Additional project-specific regex patterns to redact. Each pattern is matched against each output line; matching text is replaced with a [REDACTED:<reason>] marker. Built-in patterns (JWT, AWS keys, bearer tokens) are always applied when enabled=true.

adapter.description: orchestrator and gate-resolver must share the operator's interactive session; they cannot dispatch to a subprocess.

Public CLI adapter used for this role. Use claude or codex for subprocess roles; in-session is rejected by woof dispatch. Dispatch runs in trusted-local mode and relies on commit-safety checks before changes land, not on runtime restriction by Woof.

harness.description: Legacy route spelling. cld maps to the public claude adapter; cod maps to the public codex adapter.

think.description: Legacy cld wrapper hint retained for schema compatibility. Public adapters use effort instead.

effort.description: Per-invocation reasoning effort. Claude maps this to --effort <level>; Codex maps low/medium/high/xhigh to -c model_reasoning_effort="<level>".

mcp.description: Claude MCP server names. Empty array or omission means no MCP servers. Names must resolve through top-level mcp_servers.

flags.description: Additional adapter-specific args appended after Woof's owned options.

command.description: Executable name for a stdio MCP server. Prefer PATH-resolved command names or portable home-relative paths.

cwd.description: Optional working directory. Use project-relative or home-relative paths; host-specific absolute paths are rejected by dispatch.
